# VO2 Max Calculator

> Free VO2 max calculator using 17 validated field tests (Cooper 12-minute run, 1.5-mile run, Rockport walk, YMCA cycle, Åstrand-Rhyming, Bruce treadmill, Harvard step test, and more). Percentile norms sourced from the Cooper Institute's Aerobics Center Longitudinal Study — the same dataset behind ACSM Guidelines for Exercise Testing and Prescription, 11th edition, Table 4.7. Results in ml/kg/min with age- and sex-adjusted percentile and fitness category (Poor / Fair / Average / Good / Excellent / Superior).

Spelling used site-wide: "VO2 max" (two words, no subscript).

## Core tools

- [VO2 Max Calculator](/): Master calculator — pick any of 17 tests, enter inputs, get VO2 max in ml/kg/min plus percentile and category
- [Percentile Calculator](/percentile-calculator/): Enter a known VO2 max + age + sex, get percentile rank
- [VO2 Max Chart by Age and Sex](/chart/): Complete Cooper Institute percentile tables, men and women, ages 20–79

## Reference

- [What is a good VO2 max?](/good-vo2-max/): Category thresholds, context by age and sex
- [What is a good VO2 max by age?](/good-vo2-max/by-age/): Age-adjusted thresholds
- [VO2 max norms by age](/by-age/): Decade-by-decade tables (20s, 30s, 40s, 50s, 60s, 70s)
- [All 17 test methods](/methods/): Method comparison — accuracy, equipment, time required
- [VO2 max formulas](/formula/): Plain-text equations for each test with variable definitions and citations
- [Methodology](/methodology/): How we compute scores, norm source, limitations

- [How to improve VO2 max](/improve/): Evidence-based programming overview

- [Norwegian 4x4 intervals](/improve/norwegian-4x4/)

- [VO2 max and longevity](/improve/vo2-max-and-longevity/)
